name: ULTRALIFE CORP
business: The company designs and manufactures power and communications systems including rechargeable and non-rechargeable batteries, charging systems, communications and electronics systems and accessories, and custom engineered systems. It sells these products worldwide to customers in government, defense and commercial sectors through OEMs, industrial and defense supply distributors, and directly to U.S. and foreign defense departments.

exposures.sentence: Military applications for our non-rechargeable Ultralife HiRate batteries include manpack and survival radios, night vision devices, targeting devices, chemical agent monitors and thermal imaging equipment.
Medical applications include Automated External Defibrillators, infusion pumps, wearable patient monitoring and telemetry systems.
Oil and gas applications include battery packs for downhole and directional drilling applications such as Measurement While Drilling and Logging While Drilling and pipeline inspection and monitoring.
Our Multi-Kilowatt Module Lithium-ion battery system is a large format battery utilizable for energy storage, battery back-up, and remote power applications.
Asset tracking applications include Radio Frequency Identification (RFID), cellular, and Bluetooth systems.

Our Lithium Thionyl Chloride batteries, sold under our Electrochem and Ultralife brands as well as a private label brand, are used in a variety of applications including utility meters, wireless security devices, electronic meters, automotive electronics, and downhole drilling, geothermal and pipeline inspection devices.

Commercial products offered to date under the Ultralife brand integrate information technology equipment and power conversion capability into rugged cases, supporting use in various industries. We market these products to automotive, cellular carriers and manufacturing industries.
Search and rescue applications include Emergency Locator Transmitters for aircraft and Emergency Position Indicating Radio Beacons for ships.

catalysts.event: R&D spending increase of 10% or more
catalysts.sentence: We expect that research and development expenditures in the future, including 2026, could increase by 10% or more over 2025 levels, based on current initiatives.

catalysts.event: Battery & Energy Products backlog shipments in 2026
catalysts.sentence: The 2025 year-end Battery & Energy Products backlog orders are primarily related to orders that are expected to ship throughout 2026.
The 2025 year-end Communications Systems backlog orders are expected to ship throughout 2026.
